Chapter 59: Encountering Ash Again

Mat spent the whole day by the lake without any sight of the Dratini he had hoped to find.

Though his hoped-for company had not materialized, occasionally Mr. Caesar would patrol past with his shotgun and mock him without any malice. Sadly, the gap between the rugged, wrinkled face of the older man and the adorable Pokémon was just too large, and his awkward attempts at kindness didn't comfort Mat's tangled heart.

It was getting late, and there was a festival at the Pokémon Sanctuary organized by the Larimi tribe the next day. He planned to join the race with Tyranitar, so he decided to rest early tonight to save energy for tomorrow. As Mat packed up his camping gear, he sighed about not having the super lucky physique of the protagonist.

Yet the day hadn’t been entirely without gain. Besides the regular training, Mat and his six Pokémon, including a Ditto on loan from Mr. Caesar, held a cookout and ate their fill, resulting in a highly satisfying and pleasant day.

Considering that fate was not on his side regarding Dratini, Mat decided not to force it. Perhaps Dratini was not meant for him. With adjusted expectations, he crouched down to bid farewell to Ditto, “It's getting late today. Thank you for your company. I’ll be watching the Larimi tribe's race tomorrow, so I probably won't be able to come here in the morning.”

"Mew~" Ditto clearly looked disappointed, tilting its head pitifully with dewy eyes full of tears. After a pause, it transformed into a water-blue Dratini with a "bang" and looked at him with a curious tilt of its head, "Mew~"

Mat couldn't help but smile, touching the shiny pearl-like sphere on its chin, "Not to worry, I'll pass by here tomorrow afternoon, so we can meet again."

Just passing through meant that he would leave after walking by, not staying for the entire day as he had before. By saying this, Ditto was now sobbing pitifully. With a forlorn glance at Mat, Ditto turned limp on the ground, feigning death without moving a muscle.

Mat quickly tried to console it, reassuringly saying, “It's fine, it's fine... I plan to become a Pokémon researcher in the future. Besides settling in Pallet Town for a long time, I’ll definitely spend some time each year visiting other places for fieldwork. We’ll meet each other again for sure.”

He felt a bit sad at the end of his words. After spending two days together, they had certainly developed a bond. Mat held Ditto in his hands, squeezing its soft yet resilient body and promised solemnly, “We will meet again for sure.”

"Ice~ Ice Ice?" The Ice-type Eeveelution peeked out from behind his backpack, resting its chin on his shoulder, joining him in comforting the dejected Ditto.

On Mat’s other shoulder, Vulpix showed no interest in the goings-on, patting its now round belly and yawning, "Vulpix~" It adjusted its position to continue sleeping.

Its six curled tails glided down from Mat's shoulder, rippling in the air and creating a striking luster. Mat couldn’t help but pinch them, turning them into a makeshift crimson scarf around his neck.

After calming Ditto with some effort, and with the assurance that he’d return to the lake edge tomorrow, Mat left the Pokémon Sanctuary in the orange hue of the sunset.

Upon arriving at the entrance, Baltoy in his arms perked up, reaching out with its paws, "Baltoy! Baltoy~"

Mat blinked, "Is that the scent of food?" Baltoy had become a discerning foodie under his care. If it was this excited, it must be because of a Pokémon Breeder, likely a professional with a high level of expertise.

With the Larimi tribe's festival happening the next day, Mat had a premonition. He quickened his steps and indeed saw the very familiar trio of protagonists standing with Kazuko, who was riding Ponyta.

Kazuko, with a brown headscarf and rearranging her long blue hair, was informing Ash and the others about the festival, "We have a festival happening here lately. You're welcome to join us if you'd like. The Larimi tribe's festival is famous throughout the Kanto region. The League even sends officials to watch the races."

Mat felt slightly embarrassed hearing that; he had initially showed his League investigator ID just to make it easier to move around in the sanctuary, not considering that Kazuko might take it as an official validation from the organization for the Larimi tribe.

He coughed lightly to signal his presence and approached, nodding at Brock and Misty with an air of friendly neutrality, "Long time no see. How have you all been?"

"Mat? You're here too?" Misty was really surprised, and seeing Ash already clenching his fists, she made a helpless gesture, "Ah, here we go again."

Mat took the opportunity to look toward Ash, who was red in the face and struggling to speak. He took the lead, "Hey, Ash, good to see you. How did the P-1 Grand Prix go? Did you become the champion?"

Ash, given a cue, relaxed a little and proudly puffed up his chest, "Of course, my Primeape won the championship golden belt!"

Mat nodded thoughtfully, remembering having seen the champion's belt, made of gold, that seemed rather tacky yet elegantly designed—an appealing collectible.

"Have you... have you been here all this time?" Ash asked, glancing from Mat to Kazuko, his lips pressed together uncomfortably before asking the question quietly.

Kazuko instantly said, "That's right, Mat is the League researcher I mentioned. He has been helping me take care of the Tauros herd, solving several small issues." The beautiful girl with deep blue hair patted her forehead, sincerely grateful as she looked at Mat, "Mat also taught me how to mix the Tauros' favorite feed."

"You're too kind; that was my responsibility. I've imposed upon you for half a month, causing some trouble, so I should help alleviate your burdens where I can," Mat said, gesturing to his crossbody backpack, "Especially the gift Kazuko gave me; it was really thoughtful."

Mat's words were heartfelt. He was truly grateful for Kazuko's idea that saved him the embarrassment of showing up in front of Ash with a ridiculous basket hanging around his neck as an alternative to a backpack.

Ash, however, was not at all pleased and huffed, "Why are you accepting gifts if you already have a backpack? Do you think it’s cool to have two backpacks on you?"

"Because my backpack is now used to carry something else." Mat turned to show his own backpack behind him, "I've regained my Eeveelution that has evolved from Eevee."

The Ice-type Eeveelution, which had been dozing off in the backpack digesting its food, poked its head out when it heard its name, and upon seeing the trio, it let out a disinterested "Ice~" and ducked back inside.

"Pikachu! Pika~chu~" Pikachu, whose long ears also perked up, immediately abandoned its master, smartly clambering from Ash's shoulder onto Mat's, reaching forward to unzip the backpack and steal another glance at the Pokémon it had just caught a glimpse of.

"Pikachu was very upset when it learned that Eevee was sent away. Even though they didn't meet often, they seemed to have a good relationship," Brock glossed over the Ice-type Eeveelution's apparent disinterest in Pikachu and cheerfully explained Pikachu's actions.

Ignoring such blatant lies, Mat rolled his eyes mockingly and saw Pikachu attempting to stuff itself into the backpack. He chuckled, "I actually thought Pikachu would not recognize Eevee after it evolved."

"Pika! Pikachu!" Pikachu grinned at him, waving its little paws rhythmically, and after some effort, it managed to unzip the backpack and dove right in.

Mat opened his mouth as if to speak but held back, simply looking at Ash and the others, "I plan to stay for the festival tomorrow. Are you all coming too?"

As soon as he asked, Brock and Misty exchanged a glance, both instantly knowing what Ash's answer would be.

Without hesitation, Ash replied, "Of course, I won't miss out on such a lively and fun event!"

Mat merely shrugged, internally sighing about the disparities between bystanders and protagonists. He had been waiting here for half a month, and Ash got to participate in the festival as soon as he arrived—a perfect example of the invincible luck of a protagonist.

Actually, Mat hesitated slightly, considering whether to follow Ash into the Safari Zone, where he could certainly use the protagonist's halo to finally meet the Dratini he had longed for.
